key_insight: PICDiff stitches together independently trained diffusion models for molecular subsystems by enforcing explicit physical interactions during inference, enabling complex chemical systems to be modeled as coupled simpler parts.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route for materials and molecular design workflows to sample conformations and estimate thermodynamics for systems that are too complex for a single generative model.
method: Decompose a complex molecule or mixture into smaller subsystems.
Train separate diffusion models on each subsystem independently.
Couple the subsystems at inference with bonded and nonbonded physical interactions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstrations on peptide-polymer binding and polymer conformation sampling, with quantitative conformational sampling and thermodynamic modeling.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how accurately the subsystem models and the coupling interactions represent the full system, especially for strongly correlated chemistry.
paper_title: Stitching Molecular Worlds Together with Physics-Coupled Diffusion Models
